core_mechanism: A state-dependent (non-minimal) coupling in which a field's effective coupling is made a function of an evolving background invariant, breaking that field's protecting scale/conformal invariance and thereby driving parametric amplification of its fluctuations.
brief_reason: The abstract generates large-scale magnetic fields by breaking the electromagnetic field's conformal invariance through its coupling to the (dynamical) scalar curvature, while the same curvature-dependent nonlinear function acts as an effective self-interaction that produces the accelerated background expansion.
